title: Coordinate delegation for v1.0.4 P0 upstream fixes
description:
Use the subagent-delegation-planner skill output as the execution plan for the 15 P0 upstream GitHub issues filed from the v1.0.4 triage report.

Scope:
- mybd-7m3 / GH#3835: bd sql falls into JSONL auto-import and pegs Dolt
- mybd-2re / GH#3849: server-mode auto-import repeats JSONL on every write
- mybd-6q6 / GH#3822: server-mode JSONL import/export may lose concurrent writes
- mybd-5dm / GH#3817: shared-server env ignored when config is nil
- mybd-9v9 / GH#3818: --global can route through embedded mode and fail identity checks
- mybd-o6a / GH#3852: federation sync blocked by uncommitted federation_peers
- mybd-6lz / GH#3743: shared-server init can consume JSONL source and strand data
- mybd-8z7 / GH#3733: project_id rewrite breaks clones without doctor recovery
- mybd-l4r / GH#3722: bd init corrupts symlinked CLAUDE.md
- mybd-gfp / GH#3707: bd dolt remote add deadlocks via pre-commit hook on macOS
- mybd-7ma / GH#3701: bd prime hangs indefinitely on stale embedded lock
- mybd-xtu / GH#3606: auto-export git-add deadlocks with concurrent git fetch
- mybd-fx5 / GH#3543: bd gc reportedly deleted recently closed production beads
- mybd-jcj / GH#3427: init --from-jsonl ignored when refs/dolt/data exists
- mybd-jaz / GH#3406: embedded JSONL watcher race can delete issues.jsonl

Execution shape:
Do not launch 15 independent workers. The issues overlap heavily. Run four read-only explorers first, then implementation workers with disjoint file/module ownership.

Explorer group E1, parallel:
1. JSONL explorer: mybd-7m3, mybd-2re, mybd-6q6. Read-only in bd-main. Identify exact server-mode JSONL auto-import/export paths, minimal fixes, and tests.
2. Shared-server/init explorer: mybd-5dm, mybd-9v9, mybd-6lz, mybd-jcj. Read-only in bd-main. Map shared-server/global/init routing and JSONL source selection.
3. Lock/hook explorer: mybd-7ma, mybd-gfp, mybd-xtu, mybd-jaz. Read-only in bd-main. Inspect embedded lock acquisition, hook re-entry, auto-export git add, and watcher race paths.
4. Safety explorer: mybd-fx5, mybd-8z7, mybd-l4r, mybd-o6a. Read-only in bd-main. Identify destructive failure modes and whether current main is reproducible or already fixed.

Worker group W1, after explorer results:
1. Auto-import worker: mybd-7m3 and mybd-2re. Own cmd/bd/auto_import_upgrade.go and related tests. Fix bd sql/read-only and populated server-mode DBs so they do not re-import JSONL unnecessarily.
2. Shared-server worker: mybd-5dm and mybd-9v9. Own cmd/bd/main.go, internal/storage/dolt identity/global routing, and tests. Prevent BEADS_DOLT_SHARED_SERVER/--global from silently routing to embedded storage.
3. Lock worker: mybd-gfp and mybd-7ma. Own internal/lockfile, internal/storage/embeddeddolt lock acquisition, hook-safe behavior, and tests. Add bounded acquisition or clear failures for macOS/agent hangs.
4. Symlink worker: mybd-l4r. Own setup/init integration injection code and tests. Make bd init symlink-aware.
5. Federation worker: mybd-o6a. Own internal/storage/federation.go, internal/storage/issueops/federation.go, and tests. Commit or sequence federation peer/sync metadata so merge is not blocked by dirty federation_peers.

Worker group W2, can run after W1 ownership is settled:
1. Export race worker: mybd-xtu, mybd-6q6, mybd-jaz. Own auto-export/watch/export paths and tests. Make git add lock-aware and JSONL import/export safe against watcher/concurrent-write races.
2. Recovery worker: mybd-jcj, mybd-6lz, mybd-fx5, mybd-8z7. Own init/bootstrap/gc/doctor/recovery paths and tests. Prevent destructive init/gc/identity-rewrite behavior and add doctor recovery where appropriate.

Standard worker prompt prefix:
You are not alone in the codebase. Do not revert edits made by others; adapt to concurrent changes. Work in /var/home/matt/dev/mybd/bd-main. Edit only your owned files/modules. Add focused regression tests. Final answer must list changed file paths and validation commands/results.
notes:
Delegation metadata: coordination epic. Use gpt-5.3-codex-spark/high for E1 explorers; gpt-5.5 high or xhigh for implementation workers. Execution order: E1 explorers first, then W1 workers, then W2 workers after ownership is settled.
2026-05-10: E1 explorers completed except shared-server explorer failed compaction. Implemented W1 shared-server/sql slice in upstream PR gastownhall/beads#3865 (commit 0c150ff9c on maphew:fix/v104-p0-upstream). PR addresses GH#3817, GH#3818, GH#3835. GH#3849 verified fixed by upstream PR #3691 and closed.
[Merged from bd-7mh.3 on 2026-05-15 mybd dedupe pass]

title: Release blocker: v1.0.4 regression gate times out
description: While preparing beads v1.0.4 on 2026-05-07, the Linux release gate failed from clean upstream/main commit 8b7cbfc3bc1da3f7a30f2d0a4ec65aeeb8eb34c3. Command: make test-regression in /home/mhw/dev/mybd/bd-release-v1.0.4. Result: many scenarios passed, then TestDiamondDependency timed out after 10m. Stack showed tests/regression/regression_test.go workspace.run/newWorkspace waiting on a baseline command. The timeout left many /home/mhw/.cache/beads-regression/bd-v0.49.6 daemon start processes, which were cleaned with pkill. Per docs/RELEASE-STABILITY-GATE.md this blocks cutting v1.0.4 until resolved.
design: Delegation plan: assign to a Go/test-focused agent in A:\dev\mybd\bd-main. Reproduce from current upstream/main with `make test-regression`, isolate why tests/regression/scenarios_test.go:TestDiamondDependency hangs in the baseline workspace path, and fix the underlying regression without weakening the release gate.
notes: Handoff: release-blocking for mybd-v0e. Known failing attempt was on Linux from upstream/main 8b7cbfc3bc1da3f7a30f2d0a4ec65aeeb8eb34c3; command was `make test-regression`; timeout was after 10m with baseline `bd-v0.49.6` daemon processes left under ~/.cache/beads-regression. Verify by rerunning `make test-regression` on Linux and confirming TestDiamondDependency completes without orphaning daemon processes.
close_reason: Fixed in bd-main commit 248e594b1: regression harness now cleans up legacy baseline daemons and make test-regression uses overrideable 20m timeout. Verified WSL make test-regression passed in 1069.712s; Docker unavailable so Dolt container path was skipped.

title: Fix bootstrap non-sync actions to honor server/external mode
description: Sub-agent audit found that bootstrap planning can detect server/external mode, but executeInitAction, executeRestoreAction, and executeJSONLImportAction build a dolt.Config without ServerMode or server endpoint fields. That can make non-sync bootstrap actions create/use .beads/embeddeddolt instead of the configured server database. Add a shared bootstrap dolt.Config builder from configfile.Config, use it in non-sync actions, and cover server/external execution paths.
close_reason: completed

title: Resolve bd-main Beads remote divergence
description: Why this issue exists: the assessment observed bd stats in bd-main attempting an auto-push that failed because local and remote Dolt histories had no common ancestor. What needs to be done: decide authoritative history, recover with bootstrap or force-push as appropriate, document the choice, and verify bd-main issue tracking can push and pull cleanly.
close_reason: Active bd_main Dolt remote divergence is no longer present: main and origin/main match, bd dolt push succeeds, and the previous divergent local DB/export are preserved for audit in follow-up mybd-75c.1.

title: v0.63→1.0.x upgrade: migration backfill silently drops ALTER statements, leaves issues.started_at missing
description:
Related: GH#3363

**Symptom:**
After upgrading from v0.63.0 to v1.0.2, `bd list` fails with:
`Error 1105: column "started_at" could not be found in any table in scope`

**Root cause (internal/storage/schema/schema.go):**

v0.63.x used Go-based migrations that did NOT populate the `schema_migrations` table. When v1.0.x MigrateUp runs against such a DB it sees empty `schema_migrations` + existing `issues` table and falls into `backfillMigrations` (schema.go:135-140), which calls `runMigrations` with `tolerateExisting=true`.

In that path (schema.go:199-203), **every** error from migration SQL is swallowed — not only 'already exists' / 'duplicate column'. Then the version is recorded in `schema_migrations` regardless (line 208). Result: `schema_migrations` reports the migration as applied, even when the ALTER never took effect.

Migration 0027_add_started_at.up.sql bundles two ALTERs in one Exec:
```
ALTER TABLE issues ADD COLUMN started_at DATETIME;
ALTER TABLE wisps ADD COLUMN started_at DATETIME;
```
`EnsureIgnoredTables` (store.go:1082) runs BEFORE migrations and already adds `started_at` to wisps (via the filter="wisps" entry in ignored_tables.go:30). The wisps ALTER then errors with duplicate column. Depending on Dolt's multi-statement driver behavior, the preceding `ALTER issues` may be aborted by the batch error. Backfill swallows it. Version 27 is recorded. `issues.started_at` is missing.

Also reported by user (gh issue 3363 thread):
- `bd doctor` reports local_metadata missing (Error 1146) — secondary symptom suggesting working-set state drift in the shared-server path.

**Fix plan:**

1. In `runMigrations` backfill mode, only swallow errors that match `isConcurrentInitError` (already-exists / duplicate column / etc.). Real DDL failures should abort and NOT record the version.
2. Split 0027 into two single-statement migration files (0027a_issues, 0027b_wisps) so a wisps failure can't shadow the issues ALTER. Add a per-statement execution path to MigrateUp as a defensive second layer.
3. Add a post-migration sanity check: probe `information_schema.columns` for the known-added columns (issues.started_at, issues.no_history if applicable, etc.). If missing, attempt idempotent repair and log loudly.
4. Regression test: simulate the v0.63→v1.0 upgrade — create a DB with schema_migrations empty and issues table lacking started_at, run MigrateUp through EnsureIgnoredTables path, assert issues.started_at exists and bd list succeeds.

**Acceptance:**
- `bd list` succeeds immediately after first `bd dolt start` following upgrade from v0.63.x.
- No silent swallowing of non-concurrent DDL errors.
- Schema_migrations accurately reflects applied state.
close_reason: Fixed migration backfill error handling and added regression coverage

Added schema regression tests for non-concurrent DDL failure, already-exists tolerance, and migration 0027 issues/wisps started_at statements executing independently. title: Fix Linear BatchPush idempotency before merging PR #3654
description: Upstream PR #3654 adds tracker.BatchPushTracker to Linear. The batch engine delegates non-dry-run pushes entirely to BatchPush, so Linear BatchPush must preserve existing skip semantics for unchanged/non-forced updates. Current PR partitions Linear external_ref issues into toUpdate and calls UpdateIssue for every one, ignoring forceIDs and the existing ContentEqual/UpdatedAt checks. This can churn every linked Linear issue on each sync and interacts badly with idempotency/concurrency work. Preferred fix: either make Linear BatchPush fetch/compare before updating, or do not advertise BatchPushTracker until that semantic gap is closed. Preserve contributor attribution if fixed locally.
acceptance_criteria: A non-forced Linear sync skips unchanged existing issues in the BatchPush path; forced issue IDs still update; tests cover unchanged, changed, and forced update cases.
close_reason: Resolved upstream by merged gastownhall/beads PR #3654; contributor added skip semantics, force handling, and regression tests before merge.

What needs to be done: define explicit states including uninitialized, initialized embedded, initialized server, server unavailable, migration required, migration failed, lock held, lock contended, remote configured, remote diverged, and recovery required.
close_reason: completed

title: Map doctor/status/recovery output to lifecycle states

close_reason: completed

title: Add server and embedded storage contract tests

title: Improve coverage signal and critical-path tests
description:
Why this issue exists: Codecov currently reports weak overall coverage for beads, but the raw percentage is an incomplete quality signal because PR CI uploads a short pure-Go coverage profile while broader integration coverage runs separately. We should improve the usefulness of the coverage signal without making global coverage percentage the goal.

What needs to be done: Tighten coverage expectations around new and risky code, add focused regression tests when bugs are fixed, and prioritize tests for critical paths such as CLI behavior, storage/sync, migrations, issue state transitions, Git interactions, and data corruption risks. Coverage failures should block only when they indicate newly-added risky behavior is untested.

Acceptance criteria:
- Document the intended coverage policy so contributors understand that behavior confidence matters more than raw percentage.
- Review Codecov/CI configuration and adjust only if it makes the signal clearer or less noisy.
- Add or identify focused tests for at least one high-risk critical path.
- Avoid broad low-value line coverage work that does not improve regression confidence.

title: Epic: comprehensive docs evidence review
description:
Comprehensive review of `bd-main/docs` for correctness, evidence, consolidation, and removal staging.

Central guideline: every sentence in retained docs must be backed by one of two things:

- Observed beads behaviour, verified by running the current CLI or tests.
- Code that exists in the repository and is actually used by the product.

Review scope:

- All Markdown under `bd-main/docs`, including `adr/`, `design/`, `design/otel/`, and `dev-notes/`.
- Cross-file duplication inside `docs/`, plus root/website/plugin docs only where needed to decide whether a `docs/` file should remain canonical, become a pointer, merge elsewhere, or be staged for removal.

Required seams to make explicit:

- Architectural docs: durable structure, storage model, package boundaries, invariants, ADRs, and designs.
- Behaviour docs: CLI behaviour, config, sync, setup, recovery, errors, JSON/schema contracts, observability, and runtime workflows.
- User-facing docs: installation, quickstart, troubleshooting, integrations, FAQs, workflows, and maintainer/operator guides.

Expected review outcome:

- A documented per-file disposition: keep, revise, consolidate, split, move to archive, or stage for removal.
- Retained docs cite or derive from observed behaviour/code where claims are specific.
- Reference-like docs either become generated/checkable where feasible, or carry an explicit review/freshness mechanism.
- Duplicate/overlapping docs are consolidated around one canonical source per seam.
- Docs that do not meet the evidence bar are moved into a staged-for-removal bin rather than silently deleted.
- Gaps discovered during review are filed as beads with enough context to act on them.
design:
Use `docs/DOC_INVENTORY.md` as seed input, but do not treat it as authoritative. Re-verify claims against current `bd-main` behaviour and code before retaining or editing docs.

Prefer consolidation over parallel maintenance. Where one doc is user-facing and another is architectural/behavioural, keep both only if they serve distinct seams and link intentionally.

Use staged removal, not immediate deletion, for docs that fail the evidence bar. Suggested bin: `docs/staged-for-removal/` with a `MANIFEST.md` that lists original path, reason staged, and rescue criteria.
acceptance_criteria:
- Every file under `bd-main/docs/**/*.md` has a recorded disposition and owner rationale.
- Every retained factual sentence is either checked against observed behaviour or tied to used code, with fragile/reference claims made checkable where practical.
- Architectural, behaviour, and user-facing documentation seams are documented and reflected in the final file layout or pointers.
- Duplicate docs have one canonical destination or an explicit reason for coexistence.
- Non-grade docs are moved to a staged-for-removal bin with a manifest explaining why each file was staged and what would be needed to rescue it.
- Follow-up beads exist for any product/doc/code gaps found during the review.
notes:
Implementation PR: https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/pull/3820
Source branch: maphew:docs/mybd-pnm-evidence-review
Follow-up: mybd-xfr for automated docs freshness checks.

title: Fix TestCheckHooksDriftNotGitRepo chdir race causing intermittent failures
description:
TestCheckHooksDriftNotGitRepo in cmd/bd fails intermittently when run alongside other tests. Confirmed by the mybd-ycx worker on upstream/main as a PRE-EXISTING flake — not introduced by PR #3716.

PR #3716 explicitly excluded this test from the new CGO_ENABLED=0 puregeo CI jobs `-run` selector to avoid flakiness in the new gate. The exclusion is a workaround, not a fix.

The "chdir race" framing suggests the test changes the working directory globally and does not synchronize with parallel tests that read/write CWD-relative paths.

Required work:
- Reproduce the flake locally (run the cmd/bd test suite repeatedly under -count=N until it fails).
- Identify which test(s) collide with TestCheckHooksDriftNotGitRepo on CWD.
- Fix. Three plausible approaches:
  (a) Wrap CWD changes in a global mutex (match the existing stdioMutex pattern in test_helpers_pure_test.go from PR #3716).
  (b) Use t.Chdir which auto-restores in Go 1.24+ (if the toolchain supports it).
  (c) Refactor to avoid global CWD entirely (pass explicit path args to the function under test).
  Default preference: (b) if Go version supports it, else (a).
- After fix: include the test in PR #3716s puregeo CI job `-run` selector if appropriate.
acceptance_criteria: TestCheckHooksDriftNotGitRepo passes reliably in parallel runs (verified by `go test -count=20 -run TestCheckHooksDriftNotGitRepo ./cmd/bd`); included in the puregeo CI job; no related chdir flakes surface in the same package after the fix.
notes: Submitted PR https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/pull/3747 (branch fix/j2s-chdir-race on maphew/beads). Replaces os.Chdir+defer pattern with t.Chdir in TestCheckHooksDriftNotGitRepo and 3 sibling drift tests + TestRunDriftChecksReturnsResults. Verified -count=20 passes locally. Wider env-var leak surfaced in where_test.go is out of scope for this issue and a separate pre-existing flake.

title: Remove duplicate plugins/.../CLI_REFERENCE.md and link to authoritative source
description:

2. plugins/beads/skills/beads/resources/CLI_REFERENCE.md — separate hand-written copy. PR #3704 does NOT touch it. Plugin/skill bundle users see this stale surface even after #3704 lands.

Discovered during the mybd-p71 hand-written-doc audit.

title: Bring llms-full.txt under the same generated-from-live-help freshness gate as CLI reference
description:
PR #3704 made docs/CLI_REFERENCE.md and the website CLI reference pages generated-from-live-help with a --check freshness gate. The mybd-afb follow-up (commit ba464aa) moved the bd-version stamp to index pages only.

However, website/static/llms-full.txt still contains stale `(bd version 1.0.3)` strings — it is regenerated by a separate scripts/generate-llms-full.sh and is NOT covered by scripts/generate-cli-docs.sh --check. End-to-end consistency between the website CLI reference and the llms-full export will silently drift on future version bumps; the very class of trust-gap PR #3704 was supposed to close.

Required work:
- Decide one of: (a) merge generate-llms-full.sh into the same --check gate, (b) add an independent --check gate for llms-full, or (c) make generate-cli-docs.sh invoke generate-llms-full.sh as part of its run.
- Apply the same "Latest"-on-dev-tree treatment to any bd-version stamp inside llms-full content if it carries one.
- Verify CI freshness gate covers llms-full so a bd version bump produces a 0-file diff there too.
acceptance_criteria: A bd release version bump produces a 0-file diff in website/static/llms-full.txt unless command surface actually changed; CI freshness gate covers llms-full alongside the CLI reference; the mybd-afb dev-tree-zero-churn property holds end-to-end.

title: Move version stamp from per-command CLI pages to index only (no datestamps)
description:
PR #3704 embeds `Generated from bd help --doc X (bd version Y.Z.Q)` in every per-command page header via writeSingleCommandDoc in cmd/bd/help_all.go. Every bd version bump therefore regenerates all 106+ per-command pages with a one-line version-string change — a no-signal 100-file diff that buries real review signal and defeats the "regen as a separate small commit" workflow that PR #3704 enables.

Required work:
- Drop `(bd version %s)` from the per-command page header in writeSingleCommandDoc.
- Add the version label to the index page generated by scripts/generate-cli-docs.sh (currently the index carries only the command count, no version).
- Use the release version STRING — NOT a generation datestamp. Datestamps would break scripts/generate-cli-docs.sh --check determinism: every CI run would produce a new timestamp and the diff gate would never pass cleanly.
- Resolve the "v1.0.7 was 3 weeks ago but 10 commands changed in main since" mismatch by NOT stamping a frozen release tag on the dev tree:
  * docs/CLI_REFERENCE.md and website/docs/cli-reference/index.md → label as "Latest" or "main", with a note that exact bd version can be checked via `bd version`.
  * website/versioned_docs/version-X.Y.Z/cli-reference/index.md → carry the explicit release tag, written at release-cut time as part of the existing Docusaurus version snapshot process.
- Confirm whether bd help --all (writeAllHelp, output to docs/CLI_REFERENCE.md) embeds bd version anywhere; if so, apply the same treatment.

Result after this change:
- Help-text edit → 1-file diff (just that command page).
- Version bump alone → 0-file diff on the dev tree; only the new versioned snapshot is created at release time.
- Add/remove command → 2-file diff (index count + new/deleted page).

Note: this modifies code introduced by PR #3704 (cmd/bd/help_all.go and scripts/generate-cli-docs.sh). Best landed either as a follow-up commit on the same PR branch before merge, or as a small follow-up PR after merge.
acceptance_criteria: Per-command CLI reference pages do not embed bd version; the index page carries a version label that reads "Latest"/"main" on the dev tree and the explicit semver tag on versioned snapshots; no datestamps are introduced (scripts/generate-cli-docs.sh --check remains deterministic across CI runs); a bd release version bump alone produces zero regen diff on the dev tree.

title: Audit hand-written beads docs and add periodic freshness review prompt
description:
PR #3704 made docs/CLI_REFERENCE.md and website CLI reference pages generated-from-live-help, with a --check freshness gate in check-docs/CI. Many other docs remain hand-written: README.md, docs/INSTALLING.md, docs/DOLT-BACKEND.md, AGENTS.md, AGENT_INSTRUCTIONS.md, website tutorial/getting-started pages, integrations/*/README.md, plugins/beads/skills/beads/{commands,resources}/*.md, and others.

Some of these are intentionally hand-written (narrative tutorials, architecture overviews, ADRs) and should never be auto-generated. Others are reference-style docs that drift the same way docs/CLI_REFERENCE.md did before GH #3683 — without a maintenance prompt the same trust-gap recurs.

Required work:
- Enumerate every .md file in docs/, website/docs/, README.md, AGENTS*.md, integrations/*/README.md, plugins/beads/skills/beads/{commands,resources}/*.md, and similar surfaces. Deliverable: a checked-in inventory file (or table in docs/DOC_INVENTORY.md).
- Classify each as: (a) intentionally hand-written narrative — never generate; (b) reference-style — candidate for generation or freshness check; (c) generated by PR #3704 — already covered.
- For (a), add a header marker or frontmatter field indicating intentional hand-authored status and last-reviewed date.
- For (b), open follow-up beads per file or category for either generation infrastructure (config flag list, env var list, …) or a freshness check.
- Add a recurring prompt: a quarterly bd-prime-style reminder, a cron CI job, or a bd preflight/stale signal that flags hand-written reference docs not reviewed in N months.
acceptance_criteria: A documented inventory of every hand-written .md doc in the beads repo with classification (intentional / generation candidate / already generated); intentional docs are marked as such with a last-reviewed date; a recurring reminder mechanism exists so hand-written reference docs do not silently drift like docs/CLI_REFERENCE.md did before GH #3683.

title: Fix cgo-only test helpers blocking pure-go tests in cmd/bd
description:
PR #3704 added cmd/bd/help_all_test.go with TestHelpList/TestHelpAll/TestHelpDoc covering pure-go CLI help generation. CGO_ENABLED=0 go test -tags gms_pure_go ./cmd/bd -run "TestHelp(List|All|Doc)" fails to compile because unrelated existing tests in the same package reference cgo-only helpers: captureStdout, testDoltServerPort, ensureTestMode, buildBDForInitTests.

This is a persistent pain point. Any future contributor adding pure-go tests to cmd/bd hits the same compile failure and must build the cgo toolchain or skip tests. It also depresses codecov on new pure-go code (PR #3704 patch coverage 75% partly because the new tests cannot actually run via go test).

title: Fix Linear sync lock lifecycle before landing PR #3657
description: Upstream PR #3657 adds a per-workspace Linear sync lock and is valuable, but maintainer triage found blockers before merge. The Release path removes .beads/.linear-sync.lock after unlocking; a blocked waiter can acquire the old inode after unlock while the releaser removes the path, allowing a third process to create and lock a new file path and bypass serialization. CI also fails golangci-lint on unchecked Release/Close/FlockUnlock errors, lock file permissions, and dynamic lock-info reads. The PR description/test claims mention stale PID recovery, but current correctness mainly relies on flock release after process death and IsProcessAlive is not used in acquisition except tests/diagnostics.
acceptance_criteria: PR #3657 is either updated/fixed locally with contributor attribution or superseded with attribution; lock release no longer removes the path in a way that can split lock identity; lint is green; tests cover blocking/nonblocking contention and stale metadata behavior.
close_reason: Merged upstream PR #3657 as gastownhall/beads@9c41669c after maintainer sync-lock lifecycle/lint cleanup and green CI.

title: Fix Linear BatchPush multi-team state cache usage from PR #3654
description: Upstream PR #3654 builds one workflow state cache from the primary Linear client, then uses it for all updates after routing each issue to its owning team. Existing UpdateIssue resolves state IDs with the routed client. In multi-team configs, the batch path can send a primary-team stateId to a different team's issue. Fix by resolving/caching states per routeClient/team for updates and covering multi-team behavior in tests.
acceptance_criteria: Linear BatchPush uses the target issue's routed team state cache for updates; tests demonstrate that a secondary-team issue receives a secondary-team stateId, not the primary team's stateId.

title: Fix Linear idempotency PR #3656 before merge
description:
Follow-up from maintainer triage bead mybd-4fb.5 for upstream PR #3656. The contributor work is useful, CI is green, and the PR is mergeable, but it should not be merged as-is without maintainer fixes.

Issues to address:
1. internal/linear/tracker.go now calls BuildLinearDescription(issue) inside Tracker.CreateIssue even though the sync engine already applies the Linear FormatDescription push hook before creating. Issues with acceptance criteria, design, or notes can get duplicated structured sections in newly-created Linear descriptions.
2. CreateIssueIdempotent searches once and then calls CreateIssue, whose shared Execute retry loop can retry issueCreate after ambiguous transport/read errors. If the first mutation reached Linear but the response failed, the retry can still create a duplicate marker. Also document or mitigate the non-atomic search-then-create race between concurrent creators.

Preferred maintainer action: fix-merge or push a small follow-up on the PR branch preserving contributor attribution; avoid rewriting broader Linear sync behavior without checking current main and any stacked Linear PRs.
acceptance_criteria: Tracker.CreateIssue does not double-format descriptions after engine FormatDescription; regression coverage demonstrates acceptance/design/notes are not duplicated on create; idempotent create retry behavior is hardened or explicitly documented with a safe fallback lookup after ambiguous create errors; PR #3656 is rechecked for CI and mergeability before merge.

title: Fix-merge upstream PR #3655 Linear rate-limit behavior
description:
Upstream PR #3655 (gh-pr-3655) adds Retry-After handling and a Linear rate-limit floor/circuit-breaker. Maintainer triage classified it as valuable but not merge-ready as-is. Current upstream main equals the PR base (0fa5f210f41af9f03b1888480ce5c7ec97c03eb4), but adjacent open Linear PRs (#3654, #3656, #3657, #3661) touch nearby client/type/sync surfaces, so coordinate before rewriting shared Linear behavior.

Known blockers and review points:
- CI currently fails only in Check formatting: make fmt-check reports internal/linear/types.go is not gofmt-formatted.
- Retry-After date parsing uses time.Parse(time.RFC1123); HTTP Retry-After dates should use http.ParseTime so obsolete HTTP-date forms are accepted too.
- Server-provided Retry-After is unbounded; add/confirm a sane cap and tests so a malformed or far-future header cannot park sync indefinitely beyond intended policy.
- PR text claims linear.rate_limit_floor is configurable, but the PR only adds WithRateLimitFloor and does not wire getLinearClient/config/env/docs. Either wire the config or narrow the claim/API surface.
- ErrRateLimitExhausted currently carries reset metadata but does not integrate with the sync push loop rate-limit abort semantics; avoid per-issue warning cascades when Linear hits the floor. Coordinate with the tracker RateLimitedError direction from #3624 if/when it lands.
- Circuit-breaker reset semantics should be explicit: either stateless per-response fail-fast is intended, or reset/cooldown behavior should be modeled/tested.

Dolt boundary: changed files are internal/linear/client.go, internal/linear/client_test.go, internal/linear/types.go only; no Dolt subsystem processing.
acceptance_criteria: PR #3655 has a maintainer fix path with contributor attribution preserved; make fmt-check and Linear tests are green; Retry-After parsing/bounds and circuit-breaker reset/loop behavior are intentionally implemented or explicitly deferred; no Dolt subsystem scope is processed.
close_reason: Merged upstream PR #3655 as gastownhall/beads@a9e437a0 after maintainer lint cleanup and green CI.

title: Investigate recurring issues.jsonl normalization churn after bd export/hooks
description:
Why this issue exists: this mybd checkout is in JSONL-only mode with .beads/issues.jsonl committed to git, but running bd export or committing unrelated changes can leave .beads/issues.jsonl dirty with reorder-only churn. The observed diff contains the same issue rows moved into canonical positions, not semantic data changes.

What needs to be done: reproduce the churn in this checkout, identify whether the exporter or commit-hook path rewrites ordering without restaging, and fix the workflow so pure normalization does not leave the worktree dirty. If normalization is intentional, the hook/export path should either avoid rewriting unchanged semantics or restage the canonical file before commit completes.

Reproduction seen on 2026-05-02: after running bd export and after committing .beads/config.yaml, .beads/issues.jsonl remained modified with a 2-insert/2-delete reorder-only diff for mybd-186 and mybd-abd.

Acceptance: bd export and a normal git commit leave .beads/issues.jsonl clean when there are no semantic bead changes; if the file must be rewritten, the staged/committed result matches the canonical export ordering.
close_reason: Investigation complete: current mybd diff is semantic state drift, not reorder churn; remaining deterministic export risk is upstream memory-record ordering tracked by mybd-w16.

description:
Build the execution-side tooling so PR review is fast and cross-machine resumable.

tri-review <bd-id>: claims bd, fetches PR into ~/dev/mybd-tri/<NNNN>/ worktree off bd-main, runs go build + lint (fast), scaffolds _working_on/pr-reviews/<NNNN>.md from auto-computed signals (size, age, mergeable, checks, type), logs 'review-started: <hostname>' to bd notes, sets bd status=in_progress.

tri-resume: lists bd issues with status=in_progress + gh-pr-* external_ref. Shows mtime of pr-reviews/NNNN.md, last bd note, last machine.

tri-checkpoint <bd-id> [msg]: appends checkpoint note to bd, pushes review branch (if changes), pushes maphew/mybd so review-note + bd state propagate.

tri-submit <bd-id> --approve|--request-changes|--comment: reads pr-reviews/NNNN.md, posts via gh pr review, calls tri-close with verdict as reason.

Worktree base: ~/dev/mybd-tri/<NNNN>/. Branch: pr-<NNNN>-review. Created from bd-main.

title: Layer 2: close-on-merge sync — auto-close bd stubs when upstream merges
description:
When an upstream PR is merged or an upstream issue is closed, the matching bd stub should auto-close (mirroring the merge/close reason). Today the user has to remember which bd ID corresponds.

Implementation: a 'tri-sync' script that walks open bd issues with gh-pr-NNNN / gh-iss-NNNN external_refs, queries gh for upstream state, and closes the bd stub if upstream is merged/closed. Reason captures merge SHA or close reason.

Run from the same cron/loop as tri-pull.

title: Layer 2: smart classifier — auto-priority from rubric
description:
The existing _working_on/upstream_pr_triage.md rubric has good signal: bug-fix small recent → high priority; very-large or merge-conflicts or draft → low. Build a classifier into tri-pull that sets priority better than the current 'P3 default, P4 if dependabot/draft' heuristic.

Inputs from gh JSON: title (conventional-commit prefix), additions+deletions, age, review state, mergeable state, label set, draft flag.

Output: P0 (critical bug, fresh) | P1 (bug or perf, small/medium, recent) | P2 (feature small, or perf medium) | P3 (default) | P4 (dependabot, draft, very-large stale, merge-conflicting old).

Don't be too clever — this is heuristic, the human still triages. The win is getting decent default priority so 'bd ready' shows the right items first.
design: Delegation plan: assign to a shell/triage tooling agent in A:\dev\mybd. Work in scripts/tri-pull and keep the classifier heuristic simple, deterministic, and explainable. Map PR JSON fields already fetched by gh (title/body, labels, draft, additions/deletions, reviewDecision, mergeStateStatus, createdAt, author) into beads priorities P0-P4 using _working_on/upstream_pr_triage.md as the calibration sample.
acceptance_criteria: tri-pull priority distribution roughly matches the manual T1-T5 ranking; P0/P1 items are the ones a maintainer would actually pick first
notes: Handoff: existing scripts/tri-pull already has infer_priority; compare its output against the T1-T5 rubric in _working_on/upstream_pr_triage.md and adjust thresholds rather than introducing a heavyweight model. Verify by running tri-pull in a dry/test workspace if available, or by unit-testing the shell function with representative T1-T5 examples so P0/P1 are real maintainer-first items and dependabot/draft/old-conflicting PRs fall to P4.
close_reason: Implemented in scripts/tri-pull smart infer_priority heuristic; shipped in commit 712527f. _codex-gpt-5-medium on behalf of matt wilkie_

title: bd doctor: detect AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md divergence
description:
When AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md both exist as independent regular files (not symlinked, not sharing an inode), agents who hand-edit content outside the BEGIN/END BEADS INTEGRATION markers can drift the two files apart. There is currently no detection for this in bd doctor (cmd/bd/doctor/legacy.go has CheckAgentDocumentation but only checks for missing/stale managed sections, not divergence in user-authored content).

Add a check that:
- Detects when both files exist as independent regular files (skip if symlinked or same inode)
- Compares the user-authored regions (everything outside the BEGIN/END BEADS INTEGRATION markers)
- Warns if they diverge substantively, recommending the user either: (a) symlink one to the other, (b) regenerate via bd setup claude + bd setup codex, or (c) reconcile manually
- Surfaces in bd preflight so divergence is caught pre-PR

Zero startup-context cost (runs on demand only).
acceptance_criteria: bd doctor flags AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md divergence; covered by tests; surfaces in bd preflight

title: check-release-consistency.sh: allowlist regex is becoming unmaintainable
description:
scripts/check-release-consistency.sh line 93 joins ~14 allowlist patterns into a single grep -Ev regex:

  'go install|go list|go get github.com/steveyegge/beads|go:github.com/steveyegge/beads|github.com/steveyegge/beads/(cmd|pkg|internal|examples)|\.(go|md):[0-9]+:.*"github.com/steveyegge/beads"|go.mod:[0-9]+:.*(require|replace) github.com/steveyegge/beads|/plugin marketplace|module path|module import|module ID|bd-example-extension-go|scripts/repro-dolt-hang|CHANGELOG|changelog|historical'

This will keep growing as new legitimate exception patterns are needed. Future audits become hard because there's no record of *why* each pattern is allowed.

Suggested refactor:
- Move exceptions into an array of patterns (one per line) with a comment on each explaining why.
- Join them into the final regex at runtime (printf '|%s' "${patterns[@]}").

Not blocking — the current form works. Quality-of-life refactor.

title: doltlifecycle: embedded mode has no 'unreachable/unhealthy' state
description:
internal/doltlifecycle/state.go Evaluate() always maps ModeEmbedded to StateInitializedEmbedded unconditionally, while ModeServer checks ServerReachable.

If an embedded Dolt is corrupted, locked by another process, or the on-disk files are unreadable, the snapshot will report 'initialized' without qualification. The only signal callers can get is via the LockHeld/LockContended side-channels on the Observation struct.

Options:
1. Add EmbeddedAccessible field to Observation and a StateEmbeddedUnavailable state; treat Initialized && !EmbeddedAccessible the same way ModeServer && !ServerReachable is handled today.
2. OR document in the package godoc why embedded is assumed always-accessible once Initialized=true (e.g. because bd already validates readability elsewhere).

File: internal/doltlifecycle/state.go:60-72
close_reason: Addressed in maphew/beads PR #26 at head 1cb46190: rebased onto current main, updated PR description, added embedded-unavailable lifecycle handling, documented migration backfill behavior, and refactored the release consistency allowlist.

title: CHANGELOG note for runMigrations behavior change (d5eb2d80)
description:
Commit d5eb2d80 'fix: stop swallowing migration backfill ddl errors' drops the tolerateExisting flag from runMigrations in internal/storage/schema/schema.go. Previously-silent errors in the backfill path now surface as migration failures.

This is a safety win, but any user in the wild whose custom migrations rely on implicit tolerance (partially-applied state, etc.) will newly see errors.

Request: update CHANGELOG to call this out in a 'Breaking' or 'Safety' section, not just 'Fixed'. Specifically note:
- What the old behavior was (silent swallow of 'already exists' errors during backfill)
- What the new behavior is (only isConcurrentInitError is tolerated; others abort)
- Recovery path for anyone hitting new failures
close_reason: Addressed in maphew/beads PR #26 at head 1cb46190: rebased onto current main, updated PR description, added embedded-unavailable lifecycle handling, documented migration backfill behavior, and refactored the release consistency allowlist.

title: PR #26: Go module path inconsistency in docs/examples
description:
Public Go snippets in PR #26 (merge train) use github.com/gastownhall/beads even though the declared module path is still github.com/steveyegge/beads. Users running 'go get' or importing these packages will fail because Go resolves against the declared module path.

Affected files (paths in /tmp/beads-pr26-review):
- go.mod:1 (module declaration)
- website/docs/reference/advanced.md:143
- examples/library-usage/README.md:20
- docs/README_TESTING.md:89

The new consistency checker (scripts/check-release-consistency.sh:103) only catches 'go install', 'go list', and 'go:github.com/gastownhall/beads' patterns. It misses:
- direct import statements
- 'go get github.com/gastownhall/beads' invocations
- import paths like github.com/gastownhall/beads/(cmd|pkg|internal|examples)

Fix:
1. Either keep Go module/import examples on github.com/steveyegge/beads (matching go.mod) OR change the module path intentionally.
2. Extend scripts/check-release-consistency.sh to catch 'go get github.com/gastownhall/beads' and import paths under cmd/pkg/internal/examples.
notes: Fixed locally on branch review/pr26-fix in /tmp/beads-pr26-review (commit f45de643). Touches 8 files + checker. Verified make check-release passes; negative test confirms extended regex catches reintroductions. Branch not yet pushed — pending user decision on routing (amend PR #26 vs separate follow-up PR).

- https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/issues/3451 - injected Beads profile too aggressive about committing/pushing.

Triage from bd-r9i on 2026-04-25: these should be one policy/product bucket. #3423 asks for more canonical shared guidance; #3451 reports that the current default profile is too aggressive for standalone/non-Gastown projects, especially around automatic commit/push behavior. This needs an explicit profile model rather than one global template.

Preflight: "beads profile" found external PR #3395 (community/contributing governance). It is not an implementation of this profile work, but it touches contributor/community policy files, so review before making nearby guidance changes.

Recommended direction:
- Define profiles such as conservative/minimal/team-maintainer rather than forcing one session-completion policy.
- Make commit/push guidance context-sensitive and subordinate to explicit user/orchestrator instructions.
- Keep bd prime/setup output consistent with injected AGENTS/CLAUDE templates.
- Avoid changing runtime bd behavior unless the design explicitly requires it.

title: Regression test flake: TestCreateWithParentFlag TempDir cleanup race in embedded-Dolt path
description: Run 25413410192 on gastownhall/beads main (commit #3735) failed with: 'unlinkat /tmp/TestCreateWithParentFlag...: directory not empty'. The test logic passed; the failure was in t.TempDir() cleanup. Triggered when the Dolt sql-server docker image isn't cached (CI logs: 'WARN: Docker image dolthub/dolt-sql-server:1.86.0 not cached locally ... skipping Dolt tests'), which forces bd subprocesses to use embedded Dolt with HOME=tmpdir. A flush/journal write races RemoveAll. Next push (#3704) ran clean. Fix: add 'docker pull dolthub/dolt-sql-server:1.86.0' to .github/workflows/regression.yml so isDoltImageCached() returns true and TestMain uses the shared container instead. PR drafted on maphew/beads branch fix/regression-dolt-image-pull.
notes: Upstream PR opened: https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/pull/3737

description: The dolt.idle-timeout config key is documented (yaml_config.go:68 and now website/docs/reference/configuration.md) as defaulting to 30m, but there is no SetDefault for it in internal/config/config.go and no GetString/GetDuration consumer anywhere in the tree. cmd/bd/info.go:344 records that the daemon and idle-monitor infrastructure was removed. title: Apply the cmd/bd test_helpers_pure pattern to internal/storage/embeddeddolt and internal/tracker
description:
mybd-ycx (PR #3716) extracted pure-Go test helpers in cmd/bd into a new untagged test_helpers_pure_test.go, letting future pure-Go tests in that package compile under CGO_ENABLED=0 without per-file build-tagging. Heavy cgo-only helpers (sql.DB, dolt.New, drop database) stayed behind the cgo build tag.

The same antipattern exists in:
- internal/storage/embeddeddolt
- internal/tracker

These packages have not yet hit the cant-compile wall because nobody has added a pure-Go test there, but the moment someone tries — e.g. extending the PR #3704 generated-docs pattern, or adding a cobra-help-shape unit test that touches embeddeddolt or tracker types — they will be blocked the same way cmd/bd was.

Required work:
- For each of the two packages, inventory test helpers and identify the pure-Go subset (helpers with no cgo dependency: stdio capture, mutexes, env stubs, ID counters, etc.).
- Apply the same extraction pattern: new untagged test_helpers_pure_test.go (or similar) containing the pure-Go helpers; keep cgo-only helpers in a //go:build cgo file.
- Verify per package: CGO_ENABLED=0 go test -tags gms_pure_go -c -o /dev/null ./<pkg> compiles cleanly.
- Verify default go test ./<pkg> (cgo enabled) still passes.
- Extend PR #3716 puregeo CI job to cover these packages if reasonable; otherwise note in PR body for follow-up.
acceptance_criteria: Pure-Go tests can be added to internal/storage/embeddeddolt and internal/tracker without hitting the cgo-helper-contamination wall; the puregeo CI guard either covers these packages or has a documented reason for exclusion.

title: Decide whether to replace legacy BEADS_* env var references in user-facing docs with BD_*
description:
The mybd-hxq fix (PR #3714) confirmed that BD_* is the primary env var prefix; legacy BEADS_* names (BEADS_DIR, BEADS_ACTOR, BEADS_IDENTITY, BEADS_DB, BEADS_DOLT_*) are still honored explicitly but are no longer the documented preferred form.

Seven files reference BEADS_DB in prose where they could reference BD_DB:
- AGENT_INSTRUCTIONS.md (lines 33, 37–41, 64)
- integrations/beads-mcp/README.md:64
- integrations/beads-mcp/CONTEXT_MANAGEMENT.md (lines 30, 68, 75)
- docs/WORKTREES.md:326
- docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md (lines 522, 539, 540)
- docs/PLUGIN.md:220

DECISION NEEDED — three plausible paths:
(a) Bulk-replace BEADS_* with BD_* in user-facing docs (excluding CHANGELOG history). Pro: docs match preferred form. Con: users with BEADS_DB in their shell rc see no docs explanation when grepping their environment for the variable they actually use.
(b) Leave them. They still work; changing them risks breaking the mental model for existing users with shell rc files.
(c) Hybrid: keep BEADS_* mentions but add a "(also accepts BD_*)" note where space permits, OR document the preferred form once at the top and use BD_* throughout while listing legacy aliases in a Compatibility section.

Required work:
- User decision on (a)/(b)/(c).
- Implement: (a) one PR replacing all 7 files, (b) close with a memory entry noting the decision, (c) hybrid pattern.
acceptance_criteria: A deliberate decision is documented (in the bead, a memory, or a docs ADR); if changes are made, they are consistent across all surfaced files; future contributors do not have to re-litigate.

title: Investigate actual cause of recurring issues.jsonl dirty state after bd export/hooks
description: Why this issue exists: the original upstream-memory-ordering hypothesis was disproven. Current upstream beads already sorts memory keys before emitting _type=memory records in both explicit export and auto-export, and auto-export regression coverage was added in bd-main. The remaining unresolved problem is the recurring .beads/issues.jsonl dirty state seen after bd export and commit-hook activity. What needs to be done: reproduce the lingering dirty-file behavior against current beads, identify the actual source of semantic drift or normalization mismatch in export/hook workflows, and fix the path so bd export and normal commits leave .beads/issues.jsonl clean when there are no intended bead-state changes.
acceptance_criteria: Reproduced the current dirty issues.jsonl behavior on current beads; root cause identified and documented; bd export and normal commit-hook activity leave .beads/issues.jsonl clean when there are no intended bead-state changes, or the remaining semantic rewrites are explicitly explained and covered by regression tests.
notes:
[Merged from bd-6zd on 2026-05-15 mybd dedupe pass]
Original title: Investigate race between write commit and post-write auto-export (GH#3260)
Merge rationale: Tracks TestEmbeddedMemoryConcurrent failure with export.auto=true. Mybd-w16 investigates recurring issues.jsonl dirty state after export/hooks and mentions the memory-ordering hypothesis was disproven—same problem.
Notes: Note that GH#3260 is the upstream issue and TestEmbeddedMemoryConcurrent is the specific failing test; verify whether it still fails on current main.

title: Layer 2: epic/batch grouping for related upstream items
description:
Many upstream items are related: stacked PRs (e.g., #3458 stacks on #3453), same author shipping a series, or multiple PRs/issues pointing at the same root cause. Today each becomes an isolated bd stub.

Build: tri-pull (or a separate post-pass) that detects relationships and uses 'bd dep add' to link them, plus optionally 'bd create --type=epic' for the parent. Signal sources:
- 'stacks on #NNNN' / 'depends on #NNNN' in title or description body
- 'Closes #NNNN' / 'Fixes #NNNN' linking PR to issue
- Same author + similar title window (e.g. 'feat(session) phase 1a', 'phase 1b')

Goal: 'bd ready' shows the unblocked head of each chain, not all 5 stacked PRs.
acceptance_criteria: stacked/related upstream items appear as a dep graph in bd, not as parallel siblings
close_reason: Implemented in scripts/tri-pull dependency linking for stacked/dependent PRs and closing issue refs; shipped in commit 712527f. _codex-gpt-5-medium on behalf of matt wilkie_

title: Layer 2: bd close hook — auto-label upstream on plain bd close
description:
Currently only scripts/tri-close applies the upstream 'triaged' label. If a maintainer runs 'bd close <id>' directly (out of habit), the upstream stays unlabeled and re-appears on next tri-pull, recreating the stub or re-cluttering.

Investigate: bd's hook system (.beads/hooks/?), close hooks, post-close events. If a hook can fire on close-of-issue-with-external-ref, wire it to call gh edit. Else accept the wrapper-only model and add a guard: tri-pull warns if it sees recently-closed bd issues whose upstream still lacks 'triaged' label.
acceptance_criteria: either: bd close fires the upstream label automatically OR tri-pull warns about the gap
close_reason: Implemented .beads/hooks/on_close plus scripts/tri-label-upstream, with tri-pull warning pass; shipped in commit 712527f. _codex-gpt-5-medium on behalf of matt wilkie_

title: Layer 2: cron/loop daily tri-pull
description:
Schedule tri-pull to run daily so the queue stays fresh without manual invocation. Two approaches:
1. Local cron + gh auth (simplest, machine-bound)
2. Claude Code /loop or scheduled trigger (cross-machine via beads sync)

Should be silent on no-new-items, log when items pulled. Failure (gh rate limit, network) shouldn't be silent.
acceptance_criteria: tri-pull runs unattended daily; new items show up in bd ready next morning
close_reason: Implemented scripts/tri-daily and scripts/tri-install-cron for unattended daily tri-pull/tri-sync; shipped in commit e1a9597. _codex-gpt-5-medium on behalf of matt wilkie_

title: bd prime: conditional reminder when AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md are independent files
description:
When bd init creates both AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md as separate regular files (not symlinked), an agent that hand-edits one can forget to mirror to the other. The two files have different default content (AGENTS.md uses a richer template, CLAUDE.md uses ProfileMinimal because Claude hooks deliver full context via bd prime).

Add a conditional one-liner to bd prime output that fires ONLY when:
- Both AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md exist
- They are regular files (not symlinks)
- They do not share an inode
- Both contain the BEGIN BEADS INTEGRATION marker

Message should remind the agent that the two files are independent and to mirror substantive edits across both, or symlink them. Skip the line entirely when the condition does not hold (most workspaces) so startup token cost stays at zero for the common case.

Rationale: the rendered minimal beads section is loaded into Claude Code context every SessionStart via the auto-loaded CLAUDE.md. Putting this guidance there would tax every session forever. bd prime fires once per session via the SessionStart hook and the conditional skips the line when not applicable, so this is the cheapest place to put runtime-visible guidance.
acceptance_criteria: bd prime emits divergence reminder iff both files exist as independent regulars with the bd marker; otherwise emits nothing; covered by tests

title: TestEmbeddedInit: top-level Windows skip (initGitRepoAt uses /dev/null)
description: Reviewer of PR #3558 noted that initGitRepoAt at cmd/bd/init_embedded_test.go:70 uses 'git config core.hooksPath /dev/null' which fails on Windows. The whole TestEmbeddedInit suite is therefore unrunnable on Windows today, but there is no top-level skip making this explicit. Either add a 'if runtime.GOOS == "windows" { t.Skip(...) }' at the top of TestEmbeddedInit, or replace /dev/null with a runtime.GOOS-aware temp file so the suite can actually run on Windows.
notes: Surfaced during review of PR #3558. Discovered-from mybd-cb4.

title: configuration.md: clarify routing of output.title-length and agents.file
description: In website/docs/reference/configuration.md (landed via gastownhall/beads PR #3714), output.title-length and agents.file are listed under the 'Tool-Level Settings (config.yaml)' table. Functionally they are tool-level settings, but neither key is in YamlOnlyKeys nor in a YAML-routed namespace prefix (see internal/config/yaml_config.go:22-90), so 'bd config set output.title-length 100' actually writes to the Dolt database, not config.yaml. The current grouping is more useful to readers than splitting hairs, but a short footnote on those two rows ('written to database by bd config set, but typically read from yaml') would prevent confusion if a user inspects config.yaml after running bd config set.
notes: Discovered during review of gastownhall/beads PR #3714. Cosmetic — not a regression introduced by the PR.
